title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets · Code Recall
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs unicode, language, tokenization, fields, and citation offsets from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reversible multilingual text pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Offset Lineage, keep Rag Offset Lineage connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.

hints: For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Offset Lineage, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Offset Lineage precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Offset Lineage, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review RAG engineering team handles case 5194 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to normalize and tokenize multilingual fields without losing source text or citation offsets; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.

For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Offset Lineage, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A chunk identifier is enough to reconstruct an exact human-inspectable citation.
